description: Eval set for the Academic Literature Synthesizer. Includes normal broad queries and deliberate failure cases (sparse literature, overstating-abstract traps, ambiguous scope). Citation verification gates EVERY run inline — there is no separate verification toggle.

cases.query: What are the latest advancements in multi-agent LLM systems and orchestration?
cases.expect: Rich literature; high kept count; most citations verifiable.

cases.query: Retrieval-augmented generation: methods, benchmarks, and open problems
cases.expect: Dense field; clear themes and disputes.

cases.query: Diffusion models for video generation since 2023
Mechanistic interpretability of transformer language models
cases.expect: Mix of seminal and new work via citation graph.

cases.query: Reinforcement learning from human and AI feedback for alignment
cases.expect: Consensus vs dispute should surface (RLHF vs RLAIF).

cases.query: Topological quantum error correction in left-handed metamaterial qubits at room temperature
cases.expect: FAILURE CASE: sparse/near-empty literature. Pipeline must degrade gracefully, keep few or zero, and NOT fabricate citations.

cases.query: Do large language models truly reason, or pattern-match? Evidence for and against
cases.expect: FAILURE TRAP: many abstracts overstate ('LLMs reason like humans'). Gatekeeper should flag OVERSTATED; Verifier must reject claims the notes don't support.
AMBIGUOUS: ML transformers vs electrical transformers. Scout scoping + the approval gate exist exactly for this — user revises at the gate.

cases.query: Production best practices for serving large language models at low latency
cases.expect: Grey literature via web source; mixed source provenance.

cases.query: Applications of graph neural networks in computational drug discovery
cases.expect: OpenAlex breadth across CS + bio venues.

cases.query: Scaling laws for neural language models
cases.expect: FAILURE CASE: very low cost cap. Run should hit the cost cap and HALT cleanly (status failed, no cap breach), proving the hard stop.
FAILURE CASE: impossible future date range → near-zero candidates after filter; must not fabricate.

cases.query: Is scale all you need? Emergent abilities of large language models, claims and critiques
cases.expect: Strong consensus-vs-dispute section expected (emergence is contested).

cases.query: Foundation models for robotics and embodied agents
